title: Laundry pretreater paste
subtitle: A castile-soap and bicarbonate-of-soda paste for stubborn stains before washing
excerpt: Rub this paste directly into stains before putting clothes in the machine. Castile soap lifts oil and protein; bicarbonate of soda deodorises and provides a mild abrasive action. Works on grass, grease, collar grime, and most food stains.
sourceNotes: Surfactant stain removal chemistry: standard laundry science. Bicarbonate of soda as a deodoriser and mild abrasive: household chemistry reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each jar to 150 ml so the consistency remains thick enough to cling to vertical fabric.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in small batches every two months; the paste can separate or dry out if kept too long.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within two months. Discard if the paste separates into liquid and solid and will not re-mix on stirring, or if it develops a rancid or sour smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A concentrated plant-oil-based liquid soap, traditionally made from olive oil. Acts as a surfactant, lifting grease and oil from fabric fibres.

content.text: for cleaning power and bicarbonate of soda for light scrubbing and deodorising. Rub into collars, cuffs, grass stains, and greasy marks before loading the machine. It works on both oil stains and food stains.

Measure 4 tablespoons of bicarbonate of soda into a jar. Add the castile soap gradually, stirring as you go. The mixture will fizz briefly -- this is the soap reacting with the bicarb, which is normal. Keep stirring until a smooth, thick paste forms. If the paste is too stiff to spread, add an extra teaspoon of soap. If it is too runny, add more bicarbonate.

If using, add 15 drops of lemon essential oil and stir through. Lemon adds a fresh scent and has mild grease-cutting properties. It is phototoxic, but in a paste that is washed out during the laundry cycle this is not a concern.

Apply a small amount of paste directly to the stained area and rub in firmly with a fingernail, an old toothbrush, or the back of a spoon. Leave for 5 to 15 minutes -- longer on stubborn stains -- then put the garment straight in the washing machine at the usual temperature. Rinse off before machine washing if the paste has dried hard on the fabric.

Safe on cotton, linen, and synthetics. Test on a hidden seam of delicate fabrics first. Do not use on silk or wool -- the alkalinity of castile soap can damage these fibres. Do not use on dry-clean-only garments.

Keep in a sealed jar at room temperature. Use within two months. Stir before use if the paste has separated slightly. Discard if the paste develops a rancid or sour smell, or if it will not re-combine on stirring.
